entries.riskLabel: Neutral · range-bound bounce stalling below $65k; BI hike + Iran the macro shifts
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Range mode. A week since the $63k reclaim, crypto is stalling below $65k follow-through: BTC $63,804 (+1.2%), ETH $1,727 (+1.4%), sideways consolidation rather than trend repair. Notable shifts: Bank Indonesia hiked +25bp (75bp cumulative since May) to defend the rupiah — supports IDX recovery; INTC +10.6% on Trump announcing Intel will produce Apple chips in US (chip-repatriation narrative); HSTECH snapped its 2-session rally on profit-taking. 10Y holds 4.55%, Fed funds 3.50–3.75% unchanged. entries.riskLabel: Neutral defensive — bounce active, $63k BTC reclaim achieved, macro mixed
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Bounce engaged. Crypto has recovered some of the cascade: BTC back at $63,393 (+2.5%, range $61–63.7k) — the $63k reclaim trigger from last week's Watch list has fired. ETH $1,682 (+3.3%) — bounced ~$130 off the $1,504 low. Macro state (12 Jun): 10Y UST 4.55% (up 7bps), DXY 100.06 (firm), Fed Funds 3.50–3.75% (unchanged 3 meetings, Dec hike still priced), May CPI YoY 4.2% (accelerated — fastest in 3 years on energy). Iran de-escalation news still mixed. Not yet a confirmed turn — needs follow-th

entries.riskLabel: Risk-off — crypto cascade in motion, $70k psychological broken, Iran escalation
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Crypto cascade. BTC broke below the $70k psychological to $67.2k (−5.9%, low $66.9k) — first time in ~2 months. ETH back below $2,000 (low ~$1,965), SOL crushed −6% to $75.9 (OI +11% = fresh shorts), alts in liquidation mode. Trigger stack: Strategy disclosed its first BTC sale in ~4 years (32 BTC ≈ $2.5M — tiny $ but the 'never sell' narrative broke) + Iran/Hormuz escalation headlines + ~$1B total liquidations + technical stops cascading. Gold flat — not catching haven bid even on this. The cry

entries.riskLabel: Defensive — crypto broke down, equities decoupled at records, Iran/oil flipping
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Regime split. Crypto bear-case triggered: BTC lost $72k support to $71.4k (−2.9%), ETH back below $2,000 (−1.6%), alts soft, gold also down −1.1% (no clean haven bid). BUT US equities decoupled at new record highs on NVDA (+2%, new PC chip) — AI mega-cap leadership. Oil +5% on Iran/Hormuz tension re-emerging (reversing the de-escalation thesis). PCE (May 28) mixed — MoM softer than expected (0.4% vs 0.5%), but YoY headline 3.8% / core 3.3% are hottest since 2023. Crypto-equity divergence rarely

entries.riskLabel: Neutral · quiet consolidation — Sunday liquidity; $74.2k reclaim still pending
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Quiet Sunday consolidation: BTC +0.6% to $73.85k (range $73.4–74.2k), ETH +0.6% through $2,000 cleanly, SOL +0.6%, XRP −0.3% (mild profit-take), gold flat. Weekend volumes light (~$220M BTC vs $585M Saturday) but OI continues to drift lower across crypto — deleveraging trend intact, no euphoric long-stacking. No confirmed trend repair until BTC reclaims $74.2k; PCE remains the swing event into next week.

entries.riskLabel: Neutral · range mode — deleveraging completing; awaiting BTC $74.2k reclaim + PCE
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Modest weekend follow-through to Friday's bounce: BTC +0.6% to $73.6k (range $72.4–74.2k), ETH leading +1.0%, gold incremental bid +0.5%. Big positioning cleanup across alts (SOL OI −8.6%, XRP OI −10%) — healthy deleveraging reset, not euphoric long-stacking. Friday's MSCI rebalance is behind us; equities closed for the weekend. No confirmed trend repair until BTC reclaims $74.2k; PCE remains the swing event.

entries.riskLabel: Neutral, leaning constructive — short-covering bounce; awaiting BTC $74k reclaim + PCE
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Bear-case from yesterday is partially unwinding: BTC bouncing +0.7% to $73.7k (held the $72k support), ETH leading +1.5%, gold ripping +2.9% on safe-haven flows. OI down across crypto while price up = clean short-covering bounce. Today is MSCI rebalance day — IDX peak forced selling live now.

entries.riskLabel: Defensive bias — crypto broke down despite falling yields; awaiting PCE
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Crypto broke down: BTC lost the $74.2k 'bear-trigger' from yesterday's note → $72,900 (−2.8%), ETH worse at $1,987 (−3.4%). The dovish-Fed + Iran de-escalation backdrop (10Y ~4.48%) hasn't been enough — risk-off is leaking out via crypto first. US equities still near records, gold catching a slight safe-haven bid. PCE remains the swing event.

entries.riskLabel: Neutral, tilting constructive — the rates headwind is reversing
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: The regime is turning: a newly sworn-in, easing-leaning Fed (Warsh) + US–Iran de-escalation is pulling yields down (10Y ~4.48%, a 2-week low) and pricing out the hikes that hammered crypto. Crypto is basing, US equities at records. Offsets: mainland-China tech/semi selling, Indonesia's 29 May MSCI outflow cliff, and the PCE inflation print as the swing event.
